overrides.override_summary: Requires unaudited balance sheet of franchisor entity (not just parent consolidated)
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides transparency into actual franchisor entity's financial position, which is a $50.5M licensing vehicle backed by parent guarantee

overrides.override_summary: Standard Hawaii franchise investment law filing notice; 7-day delivery requirement; no-waiver provision
overrides.why_it_matters: Ensures franchisee receives FDD at least 7 days before signing any agreement or making payment

overrides.override_summary: Any release required as condition of renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee's right to bring claims under MD law even after signing a release

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under MD Franchise Law (subject to arbitration obligation, unless preempted by FAA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Gives Maryland franchisees home-state forum option for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice-of-law for MD state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: 3-year limitations period for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law from grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Sets specific statute of limitations for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Acknowledgements in FA do not waive or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements or waive MD Franchise Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Prevents franchisor from using integration/merger clause to block state law claims

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) required under Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly extends cure period beyond FA's standard 10-30 day periods for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: 180 days' notice required for non-renewal under Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Gives Minnesota franchisee 6 months' advance notice before franchise non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Any release as condition of renewal/transfer does not apply to extent prohibited by MN Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves Minnesota franchisee's right to bring claims under state law

overrides.override_summary: MN Rule Part 2860.4400J prohibits requiring liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: May limit or eliminate franchisor's ability to collect liquidated damages formula in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: MN Stat. §80C.21 prohibits requiring litigation outside Minnesota. Franchisee retains right to submit to MN court jurisdiction (subject to arbitration).
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Chicago, IL forum selection for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of punitive damages (FA Section 14.5) deleted if required by Minnesota Franchises Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves Minnesota franchisee's right to seek punitive damages

overrides.override_summary: Commissioner determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair under ND Franchise Investment Law Section 51-19-09. Parties enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: May severely limit or eliminate liquidated damages in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Any release does not apply to extent prohibited by law for claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves North Dakota franchisee's state law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site if required by ND Franchise Investment Law (unless preempted by FAA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Potentially overrides Chicago, IL arbitration location for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to the agreement to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice-of-law for ND state law issues
